params.name: Ulysses
params.description:
On Apr 27, 1922 AD, Irish writer Joyce completed Ulysses.
Three parts and eighteen chapters make up the novel Ulysses, which is a chronological description of a day in the lives of three common Dubliners. To reflect the consciousness of the characters, Joyce wrote a lot of interior monologues. These monologues' content is all-inclusive, combining a wide range of strange and complex feelings and desires into one that comes across as incredibly genuine, giving the reader a sense of reality.
